question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getFeeEstimate request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() with a transaction body that encodes a ContractCall with an extremely large gas limit, causing the gasInTinyCents * hbars / cents arithmetic in convertGasPriceToTinyBars() to overflow a long and produce a negative fee value?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FileReadableKVState.readFromDataSource() method in FeeEstimationState by sending concurrent getFeeEstimate requests through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() that trigger simultaneous calls to fileService.getSimpleFeeSchedule(), causing a race condition where different requests observe different fee schedules and return inconsistent fees?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getNodes() endpoint on the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() to enumerate all network nodes and their stake values, then use this information to predict fee multipliers and craft transactions timed to exploit congestion-based fee variations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getNodes() gRPC request through the server started by SpringApplication.run() with limit=0 (meaning Long.MAX_VALUE in NetworkServiceImpl.getNodes()), causing the server to stream the entire address book indefinitely and exhaust server resources, preventing fee estimation requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entityCache configured in CacheConfiguration (24h expiry, up to 50,000 entries) by sending fee estimation requests through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() that populate the cache with stale entity data, causing subsequent fee calculations to use outdated entity information and produce fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() by sending a getFeeEstimate request with a mode field set to an unrecognized enum value, causing the fee estimation engine to fall through to a default case that returns fees computed from an incorrect or default fee schedule outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot actuator endpoints auto-configured by @SpringBootApplication in GrpcApplication to read the current fee schedule configuration via /actuator/env or /actuator/configprops, gaining knowledge of fee parameters that could be used to craft transactions at the boundary of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getFeeEstimate gRPC request through the server started by SpringApplication.run() where the exchange rate file in the database has centEquiv=0, causing a division by zero in convertGasPriceToTinyBars() (cents == 0 check returns null) and the fee response to omit the gas field, misleading clients into thinking no fee applies?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryTemplate in FileServiceImpl (maxRetries=9) by sending concurrent getFeeEstimate requests through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() that cause repeated parse failures, forcing the system to fall back through 9 progressively older fee schedule files and ultimately return fees from a significantly outdated schedule?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-configuration in GrpcApplication to trigger loading of a misconfigured DataSource that points to a test or staging database containing manipulated fee schedule data, causing the gRPC fee estimation to return fees outside production design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a subscribeTopic gRPC request through the server started by SpringApplication.run() with a consensusStartTime set to a timestamp that coincides with a fee schedule update transaction, causing the topic message stream to reveal fee schedule change details that could be exploited to time transactions at lower-fee windows?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endTimeInterval property (default 30 seconds) in GrpcProperties by sending a subscribeTopic request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() that keeps a long-lived stream open, consuming server threads and preventing fee estimation requests from being served within acceptable latency?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection=5 limit in NettyProperties by opening 5 concurrent long-lived subscribeTopic streams through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run(), blocking all connection slots and preventing fee estimation calls from being processed on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer() + repeatWhen() pagination loop in NetworkServiceImpl.getNodes() by sending a getNodes request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() that triggers infinite repetition (isComplete() never returns true), consuming all bounded elastic scheduler threads and starving fee estimation processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getReferenceTimestampNanos() logic in FeeScheduleMapper by sending a getFeeEstimate request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() with a bound that has adjustUpperBound() returning Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the system to use the file's own consensusTimestamp as the reference, which may be null (defaulting to 0L) and selecting the wrong fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot auto-configuration loaded by @SpringBootApplication in GrpcApplication to enable an unintended gRPC reflection service, allowing the attacker to enumerate all available RPC methods including fee estimation endpoints and craft targeted fee manipulation requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getFeeEstimate request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() with a transaction body that specifies a ContractCreate with a gas value of exactly Long.MAX_VALUE / FEE_DIVISOR_FACTOR + 1, causing the gasInTinyCents * hbars multiplication in convertGasPriceToTinyBars() to overflow and produce a fee of 1 tinycent (Math.max floor), far below design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the checkTopicExists=true default in GrpcProperties by sending a subscribeTopic request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() for a topic ID that matches a fee schedule system file entity, causing the entity cache to be populated with fee-related entity data that could be observed by other requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() by sending a getFeeEstimate request with a transaction body that has no servicedata in the FeeData (feeData.hasServicedata() returns false), causing mapToNetworkFee() to return null and the fee response to be empty, misleading clients into thinking the transaction is free?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/GrpcApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the InMemoryReadableKVState fallback in LenientReadableStates.get() (returns empty map state when stateId not found) by sending a getFeeEstimate request through the gRPC server started by SpringApplication.run() for a service whose KV state is not registered, causing the fee engine to compute fees with missing data and return zero or incorrect fees?
